instruction: Build me a unique Legacy deck that uses an unusual strategy.
output: Here is a UB Faeries Tempo concept: Main threats: 4 Spellstutter Sprite (counters spells with MV equal to your Faerie count), 4 Brazen Borrower (bounce + 3/1 flash flyer), 2 Vendilion Clique (hand disruption + flying clock). Support: 4 Brainstorm, 4 Ponder, 4 Force of Will, 3 Daze, 3 Thoughtseize, 4 Orcish Bowmasters, 2 Spell Pierce, 2 Fatal Push. Lands: 4 Underground Sea, 4 Polluted Delta, 3 Misty Rainforest, 4 Wasteland, 2 Island, 1 Swamp, 2 Mutavault (becomes a Faerie for Spellstutter). The idea: all your threats have flash, so you never tap out on your own turn. Spellstutter gets better in multiples. Not tier 1 but a unique angle that leverages tribal synergies with Legacy's best disruption.

instruction: I want to build a unique black-based Legacy deck that is not Dimir Tempo.
output: Mono-Black Pox control: 4 Smallpox (both players sacrifice a creature, discard, sacrifice a land — devastating vs tempo), 4 Hymn to Tourach (2-for-1 hand destruction), 4 Thoughtseize, 4 Dark Ritual (powers out early Hymns and Liliana), 3 Liliana of the Veil (repeated discard and sacrifice), 2 Toxic Deluge (board wipe), 3 Tourach Dread Cantor, 2 Nether Spirit (recurring threat that comes back from the graveyard if it is the only creature there), 4 Wasteland, 4 Rishadan Port (additional mana denial), 2 Urborg Tomb of Yawgmoth (makes Wasteland and Port tap for black), 4 Mishra's Factory (creature-land for Smallpox), 10 Swamp, 2 Castle Locthwain, 2 Bojuka Bog. The strategy: strip their hand, their board, and their mana simultaneously. You win with inevitability from recursive threats while they have nothing.

instruction: Build me a Legacy deck that uses lands as its primary win condition but is different from the traditional Lands deck.
output: RG Aggressive Lands: Instead of the slow Life from the Loam engine, go aggressive with land-based damage. 4 Valakut the Molten Pinnacle, 3 Primeval Titan, 4 Explore, 4 Exploration, 4 Sakura-Tribe Elder, 2 Ramunap Excavator, 4 Green Sun's Zenith (finds Titan, Elder, Excavator), 3 Crop Rotation (instant-speed Valakut or utility land), 4 Ancient Stirrings, 2 Blast Zone, 4 Wasteland, 4 Taiga, 2 Stomping Ground, 6 Mountain, 4 Forest, 2 Windswept Heath, 3 Wooded Foothills. Sideboard: 3 Pyroblast, 2 Collector Ouphe, 3 Endurance, 2 Force of Vigor, 2 Red Elemental Blast, 2 Choke, 1 Karakas. The idea: ramp into Primeval Titan, fetch Valakut + Mountains, and burn them out with land-based damage triggers.

instruction: I want to build a Legacy deck that nobody expects but can win tournaments.
output: Enchantress is a sleeper strategy at under 1% meta share. Core: 4 Enchantress's Presence (draw a card for each enchantment you cast), 4 Argothian Enchantress (same effect on a creature, has shroud), 4 Sythis Harvest's Hand (same effect but with lifegain), 4 Utopia Sprawl (mana + cantrip with Enchantress), 4 Wild Growth (same), 4 Solitary Confinement (you cannot be attacked or targeted — you skip your draw step but Enchantress effects replace drawing), 2 Rest in Peace (graveyard hate that is also an enchantment cantrip), 3 Destiny Spinner (uncounterable enchantments), 2 Emrakul the Aeons Torn (win condition shuffled back by Rest in Peace loop). Lands: 4 Serra's Sanctum (taps for W per enchantment you control), 4 Windswept Heath, 6 Forest, 2 Plains, 2 Savannah. The advantage: nobody sideboards for Enchantress because nobody plays it, so you face minimal hate in games 2-3.
